creator: bugs-freebsd-org916@ketas.si.pri.ee
text:
oh i already got kevans on irc on this. it's he who suggested kasan kernel to see if i'm right about memory

13.4 is not eol until end of june, 13.3 is

and yes, i had this problem, you had this problem. now we can reproduce it by flooding the data through the ports

i'm not that good either in actually fixing it

or even finding the cause, which i suspect is not bw. esp how it also jumped the consoles. nevermind the different buffers (i -> o)

and it's indeed happening on amd64 too

i might have found it due fuzzing. first by accident, then by delibetate data transfer

maybe we should "vandalize" the kernel / userland more. who knows what will come out

unrelatedly, TIOCSTI is not really needed and has been used to mess with things decades. small use it has, those can turn it back on and shoot themselves into foot if they want

but yeah, imagine this totally cursed device that causes rce in your host. previously we have talked everywhere how fs codes should not trust what comes from devices. actually every external device is a risk

this is over my head so yeah a kernel hacker is needed. with or without beard and / or grey hair. i get strands of white tho
